response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine developer-tooling experience — including work on the LabWindows/CVI C IDE at National Instruments and authoring test frameworks and code generators — but the role's core requirements are not met. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms; Java appears only peripherally, and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise, which are the non-negotiable foundations of this enterprise JVM tooling position.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus authored developer tools, test runners, and MCP code generators
Proven low-latency, high-scale systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, workshops, and open-source maintainership
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated (secondary language on robotics projects circa 2016-2018)
No evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js product engineering
